Promotion Requirements - CSE at JKKNIU
To be promoted to the next academic year, students must meet specific CGPA thresholds: 2.00 to move from 1st to 2nd year, 2.25 for 2nd to 3rd year, and 2.50 for 3rd to 4th year. To be awarded the Bachelor of Engineering degree, a student must complete a minimum of 160 credits and secure a minimum CGPA of 2.5. Exceptional students who achieve an average GPA of 3.80 or above in two regular semesters of an academic year are recognized on the Dean's List, provided they have no "F" grades that year. By the 2010–2011 academic year, the university further diversified its academic offerings by establishing the Departments of Dramatics, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Public Administration, and Human Resource Management across its existing faculties.

Campus Life, Events & Administration

What clubs and festivals are active on campus?
Various clubs such as the JKKNIU Student Development Club (SDC) and programming or hobbyist groups exist. Major annual events include the cultural festival "Kuyasha Utshab" and celebrations for Nazrul Jayanti, reflecting the university's diverse cultural environment.

What residential halls and sports facilities are available?
The campus offers four residential halls named Agnibeena, Doloncapa, Bidrohi, and Shiulimala. There are sports grounds where students regularly participate in inter-university competitions.

What security and student support services are provided?
Located on a rural campus of approximately 57 acres, JKKNIU maintains standard security measures including proctors and university help-lines to ensure student safety and support.

Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) Location
The campus of Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) is situated at Namapara Battala in Trishal, Mymensingh. It is strategically located approximately 22 km from the Mymensingh city center and just 3 km away from the Dhaka-Mymensingh Highway. For those traveling from the capital, the university is roughly 100 km away from Dhaka. The specific location, "Battala," is named after a legendary and historic banyan tree that has stood for many years at Namapara, serving as a local landmark for the campus.

Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) Infrastructure - Residential Halls
The university provides residential facilities for its students through four major halls. These halls are named after iconic works or themes related to the National Poet, reflecting the university's cultural identity: Agnibeena Hall, Doloncapa Hall, Bidrohi Hall, and Shiulimala Hall. These residences serve as the primary living spaces for students coming from various parts of the country.

Institutes and Quality Assurance at JKKNIU
In addition to its core faculties, the university features the Institute of Nazrul Studies, dedicated to researching and preserving the works and legacy of the National Poet Kazi Nazrul Islam. To maintain and enhance educational standards, the university also operates an Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC), which ensures that academic and administrative processes meet rigorous quality benchmarks.

Faculty of Fine Arts at JKKNIU
The Faculty of Fine Arts at JKKNIU consists of the Department of Fine Arts. The Dean is Dr. Mohammad Emdadur Rashed.

Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) Infrastructure - Academic Buildings
To facilitate its broad curriculum, JKKNIU utilizes six primary academic buildings. These include the Arts Building, the Old Science Building, the Social Science Building, the Business Administration Building, the New Science Building, and the New Arts Building. Each facility is equipped to handle the specific needs of its respective departments, from science laboratories to performance studios.

What You Can Do Next
For further information, consult the official JKKNIU website at jkkniu.edu.bd, the admission portal at gstadmission.ac.bd, or contact the university librarian Dr. Md. Azizur Rahman for library and e-resource assistance.

When was the CSE department established, and what programs are offered?
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering at JKKNIU was established on June 3, 2007. It offers a Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Computer Science and Engineering spanning eight semesters with a total of 162 credits, and a Master of Science (M.Sc.) program lasting three semesters with 36 credits.

What is the current student enrollment and faculty size?
Approximately 300 students are enrolled in the department, supported by a faculty of 15 members and 7 administrative staff.

The historical development of Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) began with an initiative by the Executive Committee of the National Economic Commission (ECNEC) in February 2004, leading to the laying of its foundation stone by Prime Minister Khaleda Zia on March 1, 2005. The university was officially inaugurated on May 25, 2007, and while it was originally envisioned as the first culture-based university in Bangladesh, the University Act of 2006 established it as a general university with a specific emphasis on liberal arts education. Under the leadership of its first Vice-Chancellor, Mohammad Shamsur Rahman, the academic program commenced on June 3, 2007, with an initial batch of 185 students across four foundational departments: Bengali Language & Literature, English Language & Literature, Music, and Computer Science and Engineering. It is the first university established to preserve the memory of National Poet Kazi Nazrul Islam.

The library offers automated services for acquisition, circulation, and reference management through Koha and DSpace digital repositories. It houses over 32,000 books, subscribes to 53 journals, and provides cyber facilities for users.

Free access to computing resources and internet is provided across campus. Facilities for photocopying and scanning are also available.

A central IT support desk operates within the university. Password resets and technical assistance are typically managed through the Registrar's or ICT office, with ticket logging systems in place.
